note: reject: bet cannot fire at tiny1m3m. The slow EMA has half-life ~7k steps; tiny1m3m has ~92 steps total, so the slow EMA is ~99% weighted toward init (zero) for the entire run. The paper explicitly states the gap widens with training length, so the lever only fires at horizons ≥100k steps — 2-3 orders of magnitude beyond our scale. A null is expected and uninformative (computable from EMA math alone); a win would be surprising (implying α helps through fast EMA only, which is structurally AdamW). Off-niche on scale per the prompt's bar. Re-file at a larger-tier evaluation if mechanism is interesting; not worth a tiny1m3m slot.
